our_open_deliverables.text: Send pricing breakdown (cost + timeline for Merlin Intake and Ask Merlin analytics, separate line items) to Danny Espinoza by 29 Jun for tentative proposal presentation
Send system/data requirements list (data lake access, API/integration specs, configuration needs) to Danny so DP World can verify feasibility internally and with head office

Send system and data requirements list (data lake access permissions, API/integration specs, Maximo/Oracle Fusion configuration needs) to Danny so DP World can verify feasibility internally and with head office
Send pricing breakdown (cost + timeline for Merlin open-PO vendor follow-up agent and Ask Merlin spend analytics, separate) to Danny Espinoza

buyer_dependent_items.text: DP World internal review of Merlin Intake + Ask Merlin solution (Danny's team + head office) and confirmation of fit, budget, and approval path
Verify data lake access and API integration permissions with DP World head office and IT/InfoSec, confirm feasibility for Ask Merlin real-time analytics
DP World internal review of Merlin open-PO follow-up + Ask Merlin spend analytics solution (Danny's team + head office) following pricing proposal

competitive_position.summary: No active competition. Zycus is sole-sourced and controls the narrative. Danny is AI-curious and responded positively to Merlin positioning. Current vendor stack (Oracle Fusion, Maximo, Power BI, Oracle Negotiations, Sirion CLM) is not being replaced; Zycus overlays as intelligent automation. Risk: head office may require multi-vendor quotes when Danny escalates pricing. How we win: position Zycus as the strategic AI procurement partner for the Canada pilot with global replication upside, anchor on the custom open-PO vendor follow-up agent (a 'tomorrow yes' per Braden on 22 Jun, not out-of-the-box but buildable), and land Ask Merlin spend analytics as the Day 1 quick win while the custom agent is designed.

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 08 + 12 + 22 Jun, SF Tasks 24 + 29 Jun email threads
champion_strength.summary: Danny Espinoza is a strong operational champion — active engagement, drove scope definition, delivered metrics, requested pricing. He has access to the operational team and to Himanshu (IT gatekeeper) but NOT to the Economic Buyer or head-office decision-makers. On 22 Jun he said 'double check with head office' signaling a gate above him. Champion strength: strong on engagement, developing on EB access. At risk if he cannot or will not facilitate the EB introduction.
champion_strength.champion: Danny Espinoza, Terminal Procurement Manager, Canada

sources: Avoma demo call 22 Jun: Danny said 'double check with head office' re data lake access (speaker Danny, is_rep:false)
SF Opportunity: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false, Who_will_approve_budget__c = null
No Contact Roles for Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor
SF Task 29 Jun email: Danny de-scoped Merlin Intake, showing scope authority but no budget confirmation
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified or engaged. Danny (Terminal Procurement Manager, Canada regional authority) cannot approve $250k himself — on the 22 Jun demo he said 'double check with head office' when asked about data-lake access, signaling an approval gate above him. SF field 'Who_will_approve_budget__c' is null, 'Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c' is null, and 'Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c' is false. No Contact Roles for Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. The synthesis-map fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) are all null. From the calls: pricing and commercial discussions have been with Danny alone; no CFO, VP Procurement, or head-office executive has joined. Danny's 29 Jun email de-scoping Merlin Intake ('we don't think the intake solution will streamline the current process') shows decision authority over scope, but NOT budget. The deal is $250k; DP World is a large logistics enterprise; Canada procurement reports to a regional or global head office. The likely EB is either Daren Farrell (VP Procurement Americas, per prior context) or Marcelo Felberg (CFO Americas). Neither has been introduced. Mitigation: on the 29 Jun pricing call, ask Danny directly 'Is $250k within your approved budget, or does this require head-office sign-off? If head office, who reviews it and what is the approval process?' Then executive-connect within 14 days.

recommended_moves: On the 29 Jun pricing proposal call, ask Danny directly: 'Is $250k within your approved budget for FY26, or does this require head-office sign-off? If head office, who reviews the budget and commercial terms, and can you introduce us so we align on value and timeline together?' If Danny cannot or will not facilitate the EB introduction, flag to the deal owner's manager (server-provided ground truth) for executive connect within 7 days.

On the 29 Jun pricing proposal call, co-author a mutual close plan with Danny mapping the internal approval gates (pricing review, head-office data-access verification, EB/CFO budget approval, legal/InfoSec, MSA/SOW) and their timelines to the 10 Dec close date. Surface any head-office or IT review timelines that would push the date, and agree on a forcing function (e.g., FY26 budget window, Q4 implementation target) to defend the Dec close.

In the 29 Jun proposal package, separate the open-PO vendor follow-up agent (custom, Phase 1 or Phase 2 with estimated design/build timeline and cost) from Ask Merlin spend analytics (out-of-the-box, Day 1), and confirm with Danny whether the deal can proceed with Ask Merlin as the Day 1 anchor + vendor follow-up agent as a Phase 2 or post-launch roadmap item, OR whether the vendor follow-up agent must be live on Day 1 to sign the contract. If Day 1 is required, extend the implementation timeline and adjust pricing to reflect the custom build.
